text:
Hey, I know this week has been rough, and I want you to know that really does matter. Not in a dismissive "it'll pass" kind of way, but genuinely — the fact that you're still here, still moving through it, says something real about you.

Terrible weeks have a way of making themselves feel permanent, like the week is just the shape of your life now. It isn't. It's a week. Seven days that, by definition, have an end. Whatever weight you've been carrying around — the frustrations, the disappointments, the exhaustion of it all — none of that is the final word on how things go.

And here's something worth holding onto: hard stretches tend to sharpen us in ways the easy ones never do. You're learning something right now, even if it doesn't feel like anything except suffering. Resilience isn't built in the good times. It's built exactly in weeks like this one.

Also, you've survived every bad week you've ever had. One hundred percent of them. That's actually a remarkable track record.

So take a breath. Do one small kind thing for yourself today, even if it's just a good cup of something warm, or stepping outside for ten minutes, or watching something that makes you laugh. You don't have to fix everything at once. You just have to get through today.

And you can. I really believe that.
